items.title: A binary search tree
items.caption: For each node, the left subtree holds smaller keys and the right holds larger ones — search halves at every step.

items.title: Balanced vs unbalanced — same keys, different insertion order
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Balanced["Insert order: 4, 2, 6, 1, 3, 5, 7"]
    direction TB
    B4((4)) --> B2((2))
    B4 --> B6((6))
    B2 --> B1((1))
    B2 --> B3((3))
    B6 --> B5((5))
    B6 --> B7((7))
  end
  subgraph Skewed["Insert order: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 (sorted!)"]
    direction TB
    S1((1)) --> S2((2))
    S2 --> S3((3))
    S3 --> S4((4))
    S4 --> S5((5))
    S5 --> S6((6))
    S6 --> S7((7))
  end
items.caption: Inserting sorted data into a vanilla BST collapses it into a linked list — search becomes O(n).

items.title: Traversal orders on one tree
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB A((A)) --> B((B)) A --> C((C)) B --> D((D)) B --> E((E)) C --> F((F)) Note["Pre-order: A B D E C F
In-order: D B E A C F
Post-order: D E B F C A
Level-order: A B C D E F"]
items.caption: Same tree, four different visit orders. In-order on a BST yields keys in sorted order.
